Decorations dyed with different berries and sap, similar to our pokeberries, etc. Drum head made of lizard skin (size of grey squirrel) fastened on with strips of bamboo.[CR][CR]It is used for tribal dances and ceremonies, sending or relaying messages through the jungles, also to help hunters find their way back into the settlement when caught out after dark. [CR][CR]This drum was obtained by trading three Gem Razor Blades (slightly used) to a tribal hunter at Finchaven, New Guinea, 1942, by W. Jack Gardner while with the Fifth Air Force in World War II."
Hair Comb from New Guinea. Collected in 1942 by W. Jack Gardner while with the Fifth Air Force in World War II [CR]Made of wood. Carved design on the top. Dyes were probably from berries

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]"Jaguar warrior" has large ears/earrings and possibly a head-dress. Face very jaguar-like.

Aztec effigy head VMI[CR]Quetzalcoatl/Xototl/Tlahuizcalpantecuhtli[CR]Small effigy with two heads. Both heads feature gouged eyes and mouth. Main head has some sort of head-dress. The two heads represent the twins, Quetzalcoatl and Xototl. They become one as Tlahuizcalpantecuhtli.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]A 1st stage Teotihuacan (See p.40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell)[CR]Face is prognathic, headgear broad, eyes and mouth look like they were made using applied strips of clay.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Larger than other heads. Nose large and pinched, eyes and mouth are coffeee bean style. Face prognathic. Either a simple headdress or hair goes from ear to ear. [CR]Bottom has a round hole where it may have articulated to something else.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Identified as an effigy of "Montezuma II" by a student. Website reference no longer works. [CR]Headdress is elaborate, eyes are punched(?) and a disk earplug on the right side of the head. Left side of head and nose worn away. Effigy ends in a slender neck.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Very similar to the 2nd stage of Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). However, the nose is not pinched to a point as is the style of that stage. All else is consistent. Head has a large broad headdress, eyes and mouth formed by cuts. Head also features a strip collar.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly Late Tzacualli (see pg. 316 A Cerami Sequence from the Pyramid of the Sun Teotihuacan, Mexico by Robert Eliot Smith).[CR]Eyes and mouth are slits, nose is an applique blob. Head seems to have a broad headdress.
Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Small head with blob applique nose, ringed ball eyes and a large oval mouth.
Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]3rd stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). Head has no headdress, slit eyes and mouth, and the jaw is not prognathic.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly 4th stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). Head has quite a bit of decorative detail including headdress and perforated disk earplug on the right ear (left ear is broken off). Eyes and mouth appear to impressions of strips of clay.The head also features a strip collar.
Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]3rd stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). Head has no headdress, eyes and mouth are coffee bean style, and nose is applique blob.
Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Head is covered from ear to ear with circle/curly hair or headdress, has punched eyes, and slit mouth. Nose is worn off.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Late Tzacualli (see pg. 316 A Cerami Sequence from the Pyramid of the Sun Teotihuacan, Mexico by Robert Eliot Smith).[CR]Eyes and mouth are slits, nose is an applique blob. Head has a two coiled turbanlike headdress

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly 4th stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). Figurine features a tall headdress (although detail is worn away), large disk earplugs, small punched eyes, and a coffee bean style mouth.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly 1st stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). Figurine features a severly prognathic jaw, slit eyes and mouth and a simple headdress. The nose has been worn away.

Aztec effigy head (or pottery stamp) from VMI[CR]4th stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). Figurine is a rectangle (pottery stamp?) featuring a head with mohawk hair, a headdress(?) behind, large perforated disk earplugs, coffee bean eyes and mouth and a small applique blob nose.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly 1st stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). Figurine features a severly prognathic jaw, slit eyes and mouth and a simple headdress. The nose is an applique blob

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly 1st stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). Figurine features a severly prognathic jaw, slit eyes and mouth and a simple headdress. The nose is an applique blob

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly 1st stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). Figurine features a severly prognathic jaw,  a simple headdress, no nose, and mouth broken or disfigured. The eyes seem incomplete with the right eye a partial coffee bean and left eye just a small bump.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly 1st stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). Figurine features a prognathic jaw, slit eyes and mouth and a simple headdress much like a turban with the knot in the middle of the forehead. The nose is an applique blob

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly 2nd stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). Figurine does not have a prognathic jaw but does have slit eyes and mouth as well as a simple headdress. The nose seems more pinched than a 1st stage nose.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly 3rd stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). Figurine has prognathic jaw, slit eyes and mouth, applique blob nose, and no headdress.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly 2nd stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). Figurine has a prognathic jaw, slit eyes and mouth, applique blob nose, simple headdress that has worn so it looks like a large forehead, and a strip collar around the neck.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly 1st stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). Figurine has a prognathic jaw, slit eyes and mouth, and an applique blob nose. It appears to have had a headdress that was broken off.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly an effigy Xiuhtecuhtli. Head features an elaborate headdress, possible ring earrings (one side broken, other side worn), eyes ringed, mouth has sour expression, and face has many wrinkles. Possibly dark brown paint left on part of it.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly an effigy Xiuhtecuhtli. Small heaed features ringed eyes, mouth with upset expression, nose well sculpted (not an applique blob) and face has many wrinkles. There is no headdress or earrings.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly an effigy of Tlaloc. Small head features ringed eyes, pointed ears, and mouth and nose that seems animal (cat) like. The head features a headdress that shows clouds. The animal-like features are the only thing inconsistent with Tlaloc effigies.

Aztec effigy head (or pottery stamp) from VMI[CR]4th stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). Figurine is a rectangle (pottery stamp?) featuring a head with mohawk hair, a headdress(?) behind, large perforated disk earplugs, coffee bean eyes and mouth and a small applique blob nose.

Aztec effigy head (or pottery stamp) from VMI[CR]4th stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). Figurine is a rectangle (pottery stamp?) featuring a head with mohawk hair, a headdress(?) with circle perforations behind, large perforated disk earplugs, coffee bean eyes and mouth and a small applique blob nose.

Aztec effigy head (or pottery stamp) from VMI[CR]4th stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). Small effigy with a rounded headdress with 2 rings on it, large perforated disk earplugs, coffee bean eyes and mouth and a small applique blob nose.
Aztec effigy head (or pottery stamp) from VMI[CR]Effigy with headdress similar to a knitted hat, slit eyes and mouth, and a small applique blob nose. Jaw not prognathic.

Aztec effigy head (or pottery stamp) from VMI[CR]Possibly a 4th stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). Effigy features a turban-like textured headdress, coffee bean eyes and mouth, and a small applique blob nose.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly a 4th stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). Effigy features an elaborate headdress, ring eyes, large disk earplugs, and a small applique blob nose. Much of the detail of this head has been worn away. The left earplug has broken off.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly a 2nd stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). Effigy features slit eyes and mouth, and a pinched nose. Effigy may have had a headdress but it has broken off.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly a Miccaotli-Tlamimilolpa (see pg. 314 A Ceramic Sequence from the Pyramid of the Sun Teotihuacan, Mexico by Robert Eliot Smith).[CR]Eyes are slits, nose and mouth are of undetermined shape and style due to wear. Head has a crownlike headdress (incomplete due to breakage) with one groove along it. Ears posess small perforated disk earplugs.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly a 4th stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell) headress of an effigy. Head has been broken/detached from headdress. The headress is large and very elaborate and probably depicts feathers as part of the decoration.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly a 4th stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). Headdress has been broken/detached from head. Eyes and mouth are slits and nose seems to be a small applique blob. Right ear has what appears to be a perforated disk earplug. A neck and part of a torso is still attached and appears to have a neclace made of rings.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly a 3rd stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). No headdress is present. Hair is in a mohawk as well as flattened down to the ears. Eyes and mouth are coffee bean shape and nose has been broken off. Ears have disk earrings. Jaw shows no prognathism.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly a 4th stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). Headdress is similar to a bee hive hair style. Mouth and eyes are slit like and nose is a small applique blob. Earrings may be present but wear makes them undeterminate.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly a 3rd stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). Headdress/Hair lies smooth and flat from one side of the face to the other. Eyes and nose are worn off. Mouth seems to be coffee bean shape. No ears visible and no jewelry visible.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly an Early Tzacualli (see pg. 309 A Ceramic Sequence from the Pyramid of the Sun Teotihuacan, Mexico by Robert Eliot Smith).[CR]Eyes are worn and are of unknown shape, nose is pinched, and mouth is a slit. Head has a conical headdress (incomplete due to breakage). Ears may possess earplugs but wear makes this uncertain.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]3rd stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). This effigy has the appearance of a stone mask. No headdress present and there is a depression at the top of the head. Eyes and mouth are slits. Nose is applique blob. No ears visible and no jewelry visible.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly a 4th stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). This effigy doesn't appear overally elaborate but still may be a 4th stage. The headress is large the ears may have been disk earplugs. The right ear is gone however. Eyes are ringed but mouth and nose are to worn to determine shape.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly a 2nd stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). This effigy has a simple turban like headdress. Eyes and mouth are slits. Nose has been broken off.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly a 4th stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). This effigy has an undetermined headdress. Eyes may have been ringed but are now worn away. Nose most likely an applique blob. Mouth may be coffee bean shape. Ears have perforated disk earplugs.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly a 4th stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). This effigy has an undetermined headdress due to wear and breakage. Eyes may have been ringed but are now worn away. Nose most is an applique blob. Mouth is of large coffee bean shape. Ears have perforated disk earplugs altough the left is badly worn.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly Late Tzacualli monkey head(see pg. 309 A Ceramic Sequence from the Pyramid of the Sun Teotihuacan, Mexico by Robert Eliot Smith). The shape of the head is of a cresent moon with the forehead one tip and the chin the other. The nose seems bulbus. Eyes and mouth are gouged.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly an Early Tzacualli (see pg. 315 A Ceramic Sequence from the Pyramid of the Sun Teotihuacan, Mexico by Robert Eliot Smith).[CR]Eyes and mouth are of coffee bean shape. Head is oblong without headdress or ears. Nose is probably an applique blob but is greatly worn.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly a 3rd stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). This effigy has no headdress, no ears, only a small indent of a nose, slit eyes, and a very prognathic jaw. The head may feature a depression that is sometimes seen in this stage.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly an effigy Xiuhtecuhtli. Head features an elaborate headdress, perforated disk earplugs, coffee bean eyes, mouth has sour expression, nose is broken off and face has many wrinkles.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly an effigy Xiuhtecuhtli. Head features an elaborate headdress, perforated disk earplugs (although left ear is broken off), coffee bean eyes, mouth and nose is worn off, and face has many wrinkles.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly an Early Tzacualli (see pg. 315 A Ceramic Sequence from the Pyramid of the Sun Teotihuacan, Mexico by Robert Eliot Smith).[CR]Eyes are double punched fillets and mouth is of coffee bean shape. Head has either a larger forehead or a headdress that has some ridges on the top. Nose is greatly worn. Black smudges may be paint remnants.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly a 4th stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). This effigy is quite small but appears elaborate. The ears are perforated disk earplugs, elaborate headdress (although quite worn), and eyes and mouth are coffee bean shape.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]This head appears to have a very distorted appearance. There are slits that could be eyes, nose, and mouth but they are not aligned correctly. It is also possible that it is not a head at all.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly a 4th stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). This effigy is quite small and may not have had a headdress (or it may have worn off). The ears have large perforated disk earplugs, small delicate nose, and eyes and mouth are coffee bean shape.

Aztec effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly a 4th stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). This effigy appear to have had a headdress (although it is now too worn to see), ears have large perforated disk earplugs, nose is a small applique blob, slit eyes, and a slit mouth.

Effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly a 4th stage Teotihuacan (see pg. 40 of Ancient Arts of the Americas by Bushnell). This effigy appear to have had a headdress (although it is now too worn to see the decoration), ears may have disk earplugs but wear makes it undetermined, nose is a small applique blob, slit eyes, and a slit mouth.

Effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly a Late Tzacualli (see pg. 15-16 Sullivan 2005). This effigy has some prognathism and has hair that others have associated with being a male. This head also has perforated disk earplugs, small coffee bean eyes, and a slit mouth.

Effigy head from VMI[CR]Possibly a Late Tzacualli (see pg. 316 A Ceramic Sequence from the Pyramid of the Sun Teotihuacan, Mexico by Robert Eliot Smith).[CR]Eyes and mouth are slits. Head has either a larger forehead or a broad simple headdress. There appears to be no nose. This effigy also has a strip collar and a large perforated disk earplug on the right side.

Effigy head from VMI[CR]Unidentifed effigy. Appears to have primate or other animal characteristics but has a headdress (turban style with knot) and a possible perforated disk earplug on the right. There is also a high degree of prognathism.

Effigy head from VMI[CR]Unidentifed effigy. This effigy is small and delicate.[CR]Features have been mostly worn away. The eye were probably small coffee bean shapes. There is an odd collar? projection from both sides of the face with a triangular appearance. The left side projection has been broken off.

Effigy head from VMI[CR]Unidentifed effigy. This effigy is small and delicate.[CR]Features have been mostly worn away but eyes were probably small coffee bean shapes. Mouth and nose seem to form an animal like muzzle. Disk earplugs may also be present. There is an odd collar? projection from both sides of the face with a triangular appearance. Effigy is very similar to VMNH 66735 in that they both have the odd triangular collar.

Effigy head from VMI[CR]Unidentifed effigy. [CR]This effigy features an animal like appearance with large slanted coffee bean eyes, a very prognathic jaw, and a pointed nose. No mouth seems to be present or the nose may be the mouth. The forhead is large and round.

Effigy head from VMI[CR]Unidentifed effigy. [CR]This effigy features slit eyes and mouth. Nose is an applique blob. Hair/forehead/headdress broad and tall. It may have some texture at the top but breakage makes it hard to determine.
Effigy head from VMI[CR]Unidentifed effigy. Likely an effigy of a primate.[CR]This effigy features slit mouth and punched eyes. Nose is an applique blob. Forehead broad and flat.

Effigy head from VMI[CR]Unidentified effigy. Likely an effigy of a animal.[CR]This effigy features slit mouth and double punched eyes. The right eye seems to have lost the button of the eye. Nose is an applique blob and has been partially broken off. Forehead squat.

Effigy head from VMI[CR]Unidentified effigy. Jaw quite prognathic, eyes slits, nose and mouth are distorted and hard to identify. There almost appears to be something in the mouth. Hair or headdress appears only on the left side of the head and has vertical lines.

Effigy head from VMI[CR]Unidentified effigy. No prognathism. Eyes double punched, nose an applique blob, mouth appears to grimace, and there is a round chin or possible beard. The headdress is hard to determine. [CR]Effigy is either burned or has been painted black.
Effigy head from VMI[CR]Unidentified effigy. Small effigy that is likely avian in nature or some other animal. [CR]Eyes are large rings, nose and mouth appear to be a beak.
Effigy head from VMI[CR]Unidentified effigy. Effigy exhibits prognathism, coffee bean eyes, and a high flat forhead/headdress. The mouth and nose are either absent or worn away.

Effigy head from VMI[CR]Unidentified effigy. Effigy seems quite elaborate with a large tall headdress with feathers. There is a lot of wear, making the features hard to determine but there are perforated disk earplugs and coffee bean eyes.
Effigy head from VMI[CR]Unidentified effigy. Small but elaborate. The headdress features rings, the ears have disk eearplugs, the eyes and mouth appear to be slits, and there is a small formed nose.
Effigy head from VMI[CR]Unidentified effigy. Wear and/or breakage makes headdress of unknown shape and style. The eyes and mouth are large coffee beans and the nose is pinched.
Effigy head from VMI[CR]Unidentified effigy. This effigy appears to be of a duck. The eyes are double punched buttons and there is a large bill that has been chipped.

Effigy head from VMI[CR]Unidentified effigy. This effigy seems distorted with large puffy slanted coffee bean eyes, a coffee bean mouth and broken but probably pinched nose. There is a large smooth flat disk earplug on the right side. Any headdress has been broken off and too worn to make out.

Effigy head from VMI[CR]Unidentified effigy. This effigy had a headdress that has broken off. The head appears to have a large collar that has a circle shape in the center under the chin, both sides have small perforated disk earplugs, coffee bean eyes and mouth, and a pinched applique blob for a nose.
Effigy head from VMI[CR]Unidentified effigy. This effigy has a bow tie headdress, double punched eyes, a large slit of a mouth, and a applique nose. There is a neck on this effigy.
Effigy head from VMI[CR]Unidentified effigy. This effigy has a bow tie headdress, slit eyes, a slit mouth, and a applique nose. There is some prognathism.

Effigy head from VMI[CR]Unidentified effigy. This effigy has large lips, ringed eyes, a blob nose that may have been broken, and unknown style of headdress and collar. This effigy may have been painted at some point.

Effigy head from VMI[CR]Unidentified effigy. This effigy has small disk earplugs, coffee bean eyes and mouth, a round applique blob nose, and simple headdress. The back of the effigy is partly broken showing that it is hollow.

Effigy head from VMI[CR]Unidentified effigy. This effigy is more than just a head. It has a large amount below that could be part of the body. Face is highly prognathic, eyes and mouth are slits, nose is a large applique blob, and the left side features a large perforated disk earplug.

Effigy head from VMI[CR]Unidentified effigy. This effigy features a headdress or hair with many vertical lines. The eyes and mouth are coffee bean shape and the nose is an applique blob. The face shows no prognathism.
Effigy head from VMI[CR]Unidentified effigy. This effigy is very hard to make out. The wear is extensive. Eyes look like slanted slits and the headdress was likely elaborate.

Effigy head from VMI[CR]Unidentified effigy. This effigy is likely of a snake or turtle. Head has triangular appearance and has a large bulbous eye on the left. The right eye may have worn off or been broken. Mouth is a long slit.

Effigy head from VMI[CR]Unidentified effigy. This effigy may be a zoomorph. Unsure of orientation. It may be with one orientation a deer with antlers. The eyes appear as buttons and there is another bump in the face that may be a nose.

Effigy head from VMI[CR]Unidentified effigy. This effigy has slit eyes and mouth, the nose may have been an applique blob but is broken or worn, the headdress is broad and simple with a bow tie like knot. Late Tzacualli?

Effigy head from VMI[CR]Unidentified effigy. This effigy seems to be a pottery stamp. The stamp has a lot of design including what appears to be a bird in the center. The back has a projection where one could easily hold the stamp.

Effigy head from VMI[CR]Unidentified effigy. This effigy is highly prognathic and features puffy coffee bean eyes and a slit mouth. A nose does not appear to be present. Headband/forehead is wide and simple.

Effigy head from VMI[CR]Unidentified effigy. This effigy is triangular in shape and the headdress or hair is made of vertical lines. The left side of the face has a large disk earplug with a very small perforation. The eyes are coffee bean shape but the mouth and nose are of unknown shape due to wear and breakage.
Effigy from VMI[CR]Unidentified effigy. This effigy is the full body of a dog. The eyes are protruding coffee beans mouth is a small indent

Effigy from VMI[CR]Ehecatl effigy. This effigy is very well preserved and very elaborate. The headdress appears blown by the wind and has feathers. The lips are pursed as though blowing. The perforated disk earplugs are decorated and the eyes and nose well sculpted.

Effigy from VMI[CR]Huehueteotl effigy. This effigy is very well preserved and very elaborate. The headdress has decoration, eyes and mouth are elaborate coffee bean shape. Mouth is slightly open showing a row of teeth. The nose is well defined and large. Disk earplugs are double perforated and the face has many wrinkles.

Effigy from VMI[CR]Huehueteotl effigy. This effigy is very well preserved and very elaborate. The head features no headdress and the ears no earplugs. Mouth and nose are well sculpted. The eyes are either closed or punched without a button. Face has many wrinkles and some of the lines around the eyes may be eyelashes instead of wrinkles.
Effigy from VMI[CR]Previously identified by a student as a jaguar effigy. This effigy has large ringed eyes with a button in the center, small round nose, and large mouth.
